words.definition: A journey made by airplane
words.example: Our flight to Miami leaves at seven in the morning.

words.definition: An official document that identifies you and allows you to travel between countries
words.example: You cannot board the plane without a valid passport.

words.definition: The area in an airport where passengers wait before boarding their specific flight
words.example: Please proceed to gate B14 — boarding starts in twenty minutes.

words.definition: Bags and suitcases you take with you when traveling
words.example: Please put your luggage on the scale before checking in.

words.definition: A document or digital confirmation that allows you to board a specific flight
words.example: I saved my e-ticket on my phone so I wouldn't lose it.

words.definition: The process of getting onto a plane at the airport gate before departure
words.example: Boarding begins forty-five minutes before the scheduled departure.

words.definition: The process of registering your arrival at an airport and receiving your boarding pass
words.example: Online check-in opens 24 hours before the flight departs.

words.definition: A period of time by which something is late or postponed
words.example: The delay was caused by bad weather at the destination airport.

words.definition: The official money used in a particular country
words.example: Make sure to exchange your currency before arriving at your destination.

words.definition: The place where someone is going or where something is being sent
words.example: Paris is one of the most visited tourist destinations in the world.

words.definition: The official process at a border where authorities check what you are bringing into a country
words.example: You must declare any food or plant products when going through customs.

words.definition: A stop at an intermediate airport where you wait before continuing to your final destination
words.example: We had a six-hour layover in Amsterdam before our connecting flight.

words.definition: Passing through an airport or country on the way to your final destination without officially entering
words.example: Passengers in transit do not need to collect their bags between flights.

words.definition: Sudden, rough movements of an aircraft caused by irregular air currents
words.example: The pilot asked everyone to fasten their seatbelts due to turbulence.

words.definition: Extreme tiredness and difficulty sleeping caused by flying across multiple time zones
words.example: It took her three days to recover from jet lag after flying to Tokyo.

words.definition: The official process at an airport where authorities check your passport and visa when you enter a country
words.example: Immigration queues can be very long at busy international airports.

words.definition: The area in an airport where passengers collect their checked bags after landing
words.example: Our bags should arrive at baggage claim carousel 4.

words.definition: When an airline has sold more seats than are physically available on a flight
words.example: Because the flight was overbooked, a passenger was asked to take the next one in exchange for a voucher.

words.definition: A change to a higher class of service on a flight, such as from economy to business class
words.example: She used her frequent flyer miles to request an upgrade to business class.

words.definition: A document issued by an airline as compensation, typically for a meal, hotel stay, or future flight
words.example: Each affected passenger received a meal voucher for the airport restaurant.

words.definition: Describes goods sold at airports exempt from standard taxes, available to international travelers only
words.example: She bought perfume and chocolate at the duty-free shop before boarding.

words.definition: Technology that identifies people using unique physical features such as fingerprints or facial scans
words.example: The new e-gates use biometrics to verify travelers' identities in seconds.

words.definition: Officially forbidden; not allowed to be brought onto an aircraft or into a country
words.example: Liquids over 100 ml are prohibited in carry-on luggage at most airports.

words.definition: An airline company that operates passenger or cargo flights
words.example: The route is serviced by three different carriers, including one budget airline.

words.definition: The practice of selling more seats than available, used as insurance against passenger no-shows
words.example: Overbooking is common in the airline industry but can leave confirmed passengers without a seat.

words.definition: The process of returning a person to their home country, often after an emergency or legal process
words.example: Stranded travelers awaited repatriation flights after the sudden closure of the border.

words.definition: Protection granted by a country to someone who has fled their home country fearing persecution
words.example: She requested asylum at the airport, citing political persecution in her home country.

words.definition: Goods that are illegally imported or exported, typically seized by customs authorities
words.example: The sniffer dog detected contraband hidden inside a checked suitcase.

words.definition: An official list of passengers or cargo on an aircraft, reviewed by the crew before departure
words.example: The captain reviewed the passenger manifest to confirm everyone was on board.

words.definition: An unforeseeable event such as a natural disaster that prevents someone from fulfilling a contract
words.example: The airline refused to pay compensation, citing force majeure due to the volcanic eruption.
